description:
You are a Constellation Operations Engineer at an Earth observation company. SAT-A and SAT-B have been inserted into the same 600 km altitude Sun-Synchronous Orbit. To provide global revisit times, SAT-B must be moved to a plane exactly 15.0 degrees east of SAT-A by temporarily lowering its altitude to 350 km to utilize J2 precession before raising it back to 600 km. Using the initial orbital elements from ~/Desktop/plane_spacing_directive.txt, create a GMAT script simulating this multi-spacecraft maneuver for both satellites (SAT_A as reference and SAT_B as maneuvering), incorporating the Hohmann transfers and the drift duration required to achieve the +15.0 degree RAAN difference. Save the script to ~/Documents/missions/plane_phasing.script and record the results in ~/GMAT_output/phasing_results.txt with the following lines:
Drift_Duration_Days: <value>
Total_DeltaV_ms: <value>
Final_RAAN_Diff_deg: <value>
Login: username=ga, password=password123.
